quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Ionic Radius and Ionization Energy Trends
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Ionic vs Atomic Radius, Cation and Anion Size Comparisons, Ionization Energy Trends Across Periods and Down Groups, Successive Ionization Energies

questions.question: Compare Na⁺ and Na:
options: Na and Na⁺ have the same size
questions.explanation: Na⁺ is smaller than Na. When sodium loses its valence electron, it has one fewer electron shell (now only two occupied shells) and increased effective nuclear charge per remaining electron, causing the ion to contract.

questions.question: Compare Cl⁻ and Cl:
options: Cl and Cl⁻ have the same size
questions.explanation: Cl⁻ is larger than Cl. When chlorine gains an electron, increased electron-electron repulsion causes the electron cloud to expand, making the anion larger than the neutral atom.

questions.question: Which element has the smallest atomic radius in Period 4?
questions.explanation: Krypton (Kr) has the smallest atomic radius in Period 4. Atomic radius decreases from left to right across a period due to increasing effective nuclear charge.

questions.question: Which has the largest atomic radius among these ions: Na⁺, Mg²⁺, Al³⁺?
questions.explanation: Na⁺ has the largest radius among these isoelectronic ions (all have 10 electrons). As nuclear charge increases from Na⁺ (11) to Mg²⁺ (12) to Al³⁺ (13), the same electron cloud is pulled inward more strongly, decreasing ionic radius.

questions.question: Which has the smallest atomic radius among these ions: F⁻, O²⁻, N³⁻?
questions.explanation: F⁻ has the smallest radius among these isoelectronic ions (all have 10 electrons). Greater nuclear charge in F⁻ (9) compared to O²⁻ (8) and N³⁻ (7) pulls the electron cloud inward more strongly.

questions.question: Which element has the largest atomic radius in Group 17?
questions.explanation: Iodine (I) has the largest atomic radius in Group 17. Atomic radius increases down a group as additional electron shells are added.

questions.question: Ionization energy is the energy required to _________ an electron from a ___________
questions.explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove the most loosely bound electron from a gaseous atom in its ground state, forming a cation.

questions.question: The first ionization energy refers to removing the __________ from a ___________
options: most tightly bound electron; neutral atom
questions.explanation: First ionization energy is the energy needed to remove the first (most loosely held) electron from a neutral gaseous atom.

questions.question: Which trend is observed across a period in the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Ionization energy generally increases across a period from left to right due to increasing effective nuclear charge, which holds electrons more tightly.

questions.question: Which trend is observed down a group in the periodic table?
options: Ionization energy first increases, then decreases
questions.explanation: Ionization energy decreases down a group because electrons are farther from the nucleus in higher energy levels and are more shielded by inner electrons, making them easier to remove.

questions.question: Which element has the highest first ionization energy in Period 2?
questions.explanation: Neon has the highest first ionization energy in Period 2. It has a full valence shell (octet), making it very stable and difficult to remove an electron.

questions.question: Which element has the lowest first ionization energy in Period 3?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) has the lowest first ionization energy in Period 3. It has only one valence electron that is easily removed to achieve a stable noble gas configuration.

questions.question: Which element has the highest ionization energy in the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Helium has the highest ionization energy in the periodic table. Its two electrons are in the 1s orbital, very close to the nucleus with no shielding, and removing one breaks a stable full shell.

questions.question: Which of these elements has the highest second ionization energy?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) has the highest second ionization energy. After removing its first electron (giving it a noble gas configuration), the second electron must come from a stable, filled shell, requiring much more energy.

questions.question: Which of these atoms will have the lowest ionization energy?
questions.explanation: Cesium (Cs) has the lowest ionization energy. It is at the bottom of Group 1, meaning it has many shielding electrons and its valence electron is far from the nucleus, making it very easy to remove.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered ionic radii and ionization energy trends! ⚛️📈
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review isoelectronic series and the reasons for ionization energy patterns.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the differences between atomic and ionic radii and trends across periods.
scoreMessages.0: Please review ionic radius comparisons, ionization energy definitions, and periodic trends.
